title: leptochilus buergerianus
intro: Leptochilus buergerianus, also known as Buerger's fern, is a species of flowering plant in the family Polypodiaceae. It is a type of fern that is native to East Asia and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant contains compounds such as flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it beneficial for treating various health conditions. Leptochilus buergerianus can be prepared into herbal teas, capsules, or infusions, and is often used to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
